subject: DevOps
title: DevOps at Amazon
description: A quick win: understand DevOps at Amazon.

hook.text: In 2001, Amazon's monolith was drowning in deployment queues. Werner Vogels demanded a new architecture.

buildup.text: Amazon decomposed into microservices. Each service owned by a small team with full deploy authority.

discovery.text: Apollo, their internal deploy system, handles over 150 million deployments per year. Fully automated.

twist.text: Every team runs their own pipeline. There is no central deployment team. Autonomy at scale.

climax.text: This model produced AWS. The infrastructure they built for themselves became a product for the world.

punchline.text: Amazon's DevOps pain built AWS. Internal tools became a cloud.

quiz.question: What product emerged from Amazon's internal DevOps infrastructure?
